03 · Bank Coverage

Bank Coverage Summary

Banks observed across tested payment flows.

Banco Activo
Observed in the 1xBet Pago Móvil H2H flow, the 1xBet Pago Móvil via pay.sirppayments.com flow, and the Binomo / pay.trx-point.com flow.
1xBet Pago Móvil H2H 1xBet — sirppayments Binomo — trx-point
Banco Provincial
Observed in the Melbet Banco Provincial P2P flow and in the 1xBet Banco Provincial deposit route.
Melbet P2P 1xBet Banco P2P
Banco R4 / MiBanco
Observed as the destination bank in the Mystake / pagoflash.com flow.
Mystake — pagoflash.com
Bancamiga
Observed in the 1xBet Bancamiga deposit route.
1xBet Bancamiga route
Banesco
Observed in the 1xBet Banesco / Pago Móvil deposit route.
1xBet Banesco route

06 · Payment Method Findings

Payment Method Findings

Field analysis and product conclusions per payment method, based on observed flows.

Pago Móvil
Core Fields
Phone number Bank Transaction ID / Reference
Common Fields
National ID / Cédula Document number Date
Additional / Provider-specific
Amount Full account holder name Email
Merchant-side pattern

Users appear to enter phone number, bank, and transaction ID after completing the transfer in their banking app. National ID or Cédula is commonly requested.

Provider-side pattern

Provider-hosted forms (pay.sirppayments.com, pagoflash.com, pay.trx-point.com) appear to add bank, date, and amount fields to structure the confirmation process.

Recommended minimum field set
Phone number Bank Transaction ID National ID / Cédula
Based on observed flows, phone number, bank, and transaction ID appear to be the minimum fields needed to process a Pago Móvil confirmation. National ID is commonly requested and is likely important for reconciliation.
Bank Transfer / P2P
Core Fields
Bank account or phone Cédula de identidad Full account holder name
Common Fields
Transaction ID / Transaction reference / Receipt number Receipt screenshot
Additional / Provider-specific
Transfer concept text ("TRANSFERENCIA")
Merchant-side pattern

Bank-transfer flows (Banco Provincial, Bancamiga, Banesco) appear to require the user to provide their own account or phone number, full name, Cédula, and a screenshot of the completed transfer. Transfer concept instructions appear to be specific to certain routes.

Provider-side pattern

Not observed for pure bank-transfer routes. Provider-side flows appear to focus on Pago Móvil.

Recommended minimum field set
Bank account or phone Cédula Full name Reference number Receipt screenshot
Bank-transfer flows appear to require more fields than Pago Móvil flows. Screenshot upload is commonly requested and is a key friction point. Automatic matching on bank account + Cédula + amount appears to be the main reconciliation opportunity.

08 · Product Opportunities

Product Opportunities

Recommended product direction for Venezuela payment flows.

Support Pago Móvil confirmation flows
All tested merchants use Pago Móvil as the main deposit method. Full support for Pago Móvil confirmation — including bank, phone, ID, amount, transaction ID and date — is required for this market.
Support all 5 observed banks
Banco Activo, Banco Provincial, Banco R4 / MiBanco, Bancamiga and Banesco appear in tested flows. Bank account structure and phone-number formats need to be validated per bank.
Build automatic payment checking
Match bank, phone number, ID document, amount, transaction ID and date automatically. This is the main improvement opportunity and the pattern already observed in the Mystake / pagoflash.com flow.
Keep manual fallback when auto-check fails
If automatic checking does not confirm the payment, users should be able to enter bank, phone, document, reference, amount and date manually to trigger a manual review.
Support receipt screenshot upload
Screenshot upload is required in several bank-transfer flows. Support jpg, jpeg, png and pdf formats. A receipt-parsing step would reduce manual review time.
Offer provider-hosted and merchant-side forms
Both integration models are active in this market. Provider-hosted pages provide more structure. Merchant-side forms provide more control. Supporting both covers more merchant types.
Give users clear payment instructions
Several flows require specific words in the transfer concept field (e.g. "TRANSFERENCIA"). Clear on-screen guidance reduces deposit failures caused by incorrect transfer details.
Prioritize high-ranking merchants
juega en linea (rank 1 betting, score 1.000) and deriv (rank 1 forex, score 0.9538) are the highest-traffic Venezuela merchants with no tested flows. These are the top outreach targets.
09 · Final Findings

Final Findings

Key takeaways from the Venezuela payment R&D.

Most important payment methods
Pago Móvil and bank P2P transfer (Banco Provincial, Bancamiga, Banesco)
Most important providers
pagoflash.com (emerging, best UX) · pay.sirppayments.com · pay.trx-point.com
Most important banks
Banco Activo (most frequent) · Banco Provincial · Banco R4 / MiBanco · Bancamiga · Banesco
Best observed pattern
Mystake / pagoflash.com — automatic payment check with manual fallback. Only flow with auto-verification.
Common required fields
Phone number, ID and transaction reference appear to be universal required fields. Every tested flow requires at least two of these three.
Venezuela Payment R&D Report · Internal Use Only · May 2026
